home.bio: I build digital experiences focused on performance, design and clean code.

items.name: JavaScript
items.description: My most developed programming language and the one I enjoy most — the first one I ever learned.

items.name: HTML & CSS
items.description: Markup and styling languages I use extensively. I have broad knowledge and can develop quickly and efficiently with them.

items.name: Vue.js
items.description: Progressive JavaScript framework for building user interfaces. My favourite framework — used in several projects including this site.

items.name: Node.js
items.description: JavaScript runtime for the server side. Used in college projects and others.

items.name: Express.js
items.description: Web application framework for Node.js. Used at college and in some simple projects.

items.name: MongoDB
items.description: Document-oriented NoSQL database. Loved using it, though my experience was mainly in a college project.

items.name: Python
items.description: High-level programming language supporting multiple paradigms. My first college language — built many projects and automations, though I don't use it much currently.

items.name: Django
items.description: High-level Python web framework. Loved what you can do with it — used in a Python mini-course and at college.

items.name: React
items.description: JavaScript library for building user interfaces. Used React multiple times at college and in a project I didn't finish.

items.name: Git
items.description: Distributed version control system. I use it constantly to manage versions of my projects on GitHub.

items.name: Docker
items.description: Platform for building, shipping, and running applications in containers. Tried it out of curiosity and studied it on my own.

items.name: Google Cloud
items.description: Cloud services platform I use for my personal projects.

items.name: Microsoft Azure
items.description: Cloud services platform I use at the company where I work.

items.name: Zabbix
items.description: Monitoring tool I use in my current infrastructure.

items.name: Office 365 Administration
items.description: Tool I use to administer Office 365 at the company where I work.

items.name: GLPI
items.description: IT service management tool I use at the company where I work.

items.name: PowerShell
items.description: Love using and learning PowerShell to build Active Directory automations. Used in several projects at my current company.

items.name: DevOps
items.description: Practices that combine software development and IT operations to shorten system lifecycle and provide continuous high-quality delivery.

items.title: ProfileCard-DevEmDobro
items.description: The ProfileCard project from Dev Em Dobro is part of a course with lifetime mentorship. It covers flexbox usage and card proportions, providing a great first experience for those starting with HTML and CSS.

items.title: Agency Landing Page
items.description: I built a Landing Page with HTML and CSS as part of the DevEmDobro course. Used CSS Grid and Flexbox to make it fully responsive.

items.title: Huddle Landing Page
items.description: Built a landing page with HTML and CSS as a Frontend Mentor challenge, presented by DevEmDobro.

items.title: Advice Generator
items.description: Started as a simple Frontend Mentor challenge using HTML, CSS and JavaScript. Evolved to incorporate multiple APIs, always focused on improving user experience.

items.title: Recipes
items.description: A recipe website created from a college project. Simple but functional, focused on providing culinary recipes using HTML, CSS and Bootstrap.

items.title: 3 College Projects
items.description: Three projects developed in college exploring HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Vue.js, Node.js and external APIs.

items.title: Score Histogram Generator
items.description: A college project using Python to generate a word cloud and a score histogram.

items.title: Expense Charts in Python
items.description: A college project using Python to create charts from spending and expense data.
